sections.title: Market Pulse: Tech Drives Indices to Record Highs Amid Geopolitical Tensions

items.headline: S&P 500 and Nasdaq Reach Record Closings as Tech Sector Surges on Strong Earnings; Dow Ends Lower.
items.summary: On Friday, May 2, 2026, the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite advanced to new record closing highs, propelled by robust corporate earnings and significant gains in technology stocks. The Dow Jones Industrial Average, however, closed modestly lower. Overall market sentiment was influenced by a strong earnings season, particularly from major tech companies, though geopolitical risks, including escalating oil prices and the ongoing conflict involving Iran, continued to cast a shadow of uncertainty over global markets. With the weekend ahead, markets are currently closed for trading.

items.headline: Apple (AAPL) Advances on Strong Sales Forecast
items.summary: Apple shares advanced, buoyed by a solid sales forecast and strong demand for its flagship iPhone 17 and MacBook Neo.

items.headline: Atlassian (TEAM) Soars on Earnings Beat and Raised Outlook
items.summary: Atlassian stock soared after the software company reported a strong fiscal third-quarter beat and subsequently raised its full-year guidance.

items.headline: Reddit (RDDT) Jumps on Upbeat Revenue Forecast
items.summary: Reddit's stock jumped following an upbeat quarterly revenue forecast, signaling positive investor sentiment.

items.headline: Centene (CNC) Rallies on Boosted Profit Outlook
items.summary: Centene experienced a significant weekly gain after the health insurer boosted its profit outlook for the year and reported a first-quarter beat.

items.headline: Roblox (RBLX) Falls on Bookings Forecast Cut
items.summary: Roblox shares fell sharply after the company cut its annual bookings forecast, leading to investor concerns.

items.headline: Amgen (AMGN) Dives on FDA Concerns
items.summary: Amgen stock dived following investor concerns over the FDA's proposal to withdraw approval for its blood vessel inflammation drug, Tavneos, after it was linked to several cases of serious liver injuries.

items.headline: Clorox (CLX) Drops on Slashed EPS Guidance
items.summary: Clorox experienced its worst weekly drop since 2022 after the company slashed its full-year EPS guidance and reported a third-quarter organic sales miss.

items.headline: Mastercard (MA) Declines Despite Earnings Beat Amid Transaction Slowdown
items.summary: Mastercard saw a weekly decline even after delivering better-than-expected Q1 earnings and revenue, as April trends showed a slowdown in cross-border transaction growth.

items.headline: Technology Sector Leads Market Gains, Energy Lags
items.summary: Technology stocks enjoyed the biggest percentage gains on Friday, contributing significantly to the S&P 500 and Nasdaq's record highs. Conversely, energy shares experienced the most significant drops. Communication Services and Information Technology sectors continue to be highly concentrated in a few individual stocks, presenting potential idiosyncratic risks.

items.headline: S&P 500 Companies Report Strong Q1 Earnings Growth
items.summary: Analysts now estimate aggregate first-quarter earnings growth of 27.8% year-on-year for S&P 500 companies, marking the biggest earnings growth since Q4 2021. This surge is partly attributed to the 'Magnificent Seven' group of AI-related stocks.

items.headline: Minneapolis Fed President Signals Rate Uncertainty Amid Geopolitical Conflict
items.summary: Minneapolis Fed President Neel Kashkari stated that the economy is too uncertain due to the ongoing conflict involving Iran to signal rate cuts. He suggested the next rate change could be either a cut or a hike, depending on economic evolution, especially concerning potential oil price surges from Strait of Hormuz disruptions.

items.headline: GDP Weaker Than Expected, Inflation Higher Than Target
items.summary: Recent GDP data came in at a decent 2%, though weaker than anticipated, with business investments in AI infrastructure being a significant component. Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) inflation stands at 3.5%, notably higher than the Federal Reserve's 2% target.

items.headline: Oil Prices Surge to $120 Amid Iran Conflict; New Tariffs on European Autos Announced
items.summary: Oil prices briefly crossed $120 a barrel this week, the first time since 2022, primarily due to the ongoing conflict involving Iran and disruptions in global energy flows, particularly through the Strait of Hormuz. Additionally, the Trump administration announced the imposition of a 25% tariff on European automobiles, adding to market volatility.
